message:
fix(channel): RAII guard prevents message loss during FaultSender flush

FaultSender::flush() previously manually restored undelivered messages
to the reorder buffer on each error path. If a panic or early return
occurred between draining and restoration, buffered messages were lost.

The fix introduces FlushGuard, a RAII struct that holds the pending
iterator and current message. Its Drop impl automatically pushes any
undelivered messages back to the reorder buffer, making the flush
panic-safe.

Additionally, flush now uses reserve()+try_send() instead of send(),
separating backpressure waiting from the actual enqueue. This lets the
guard track exactly which message was in-flight when an error occurred.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
